dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. People are sick. The village is falling apart. Tell me what you've heard.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: More than I'd like. Sarah locked herself in the laboratory before the worst of it. She thought she could find a cure.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Thought?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Last person through here said she sounded off. Slurred. I'd wager she caught it herself.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The lab is locked.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Key went missing the day they buried the last of the old doctors. Graveyard. That's where I'd look. And the power's out down there — talk to George at the powerstation.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anything else?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Be careful in the village. It's not what it was. Move quick.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I need power restored to the laboratory.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Lab's offline. Blown fuse, most likely. Got a replacement?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Not yet. Anne said there's a spare fuse inside the lab itself.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Figures. Bring it here, I'll route it through. Won't take long.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll be back.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: I'll be here. Not going anywhere.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: You shouldn't... stand so close. I'm not safe.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. How long?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Two days. Maybe less before I lose coherence. The synthesis machine works — I just couldn't get it powered in time. There's a sample at the hospital. Marcus moved it to the upper ward before he barricaded himself in.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll get the power on and bring the sample. Hold on.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: If I'm too far gone when you return... use the antidote anyway. Promise me you'll try.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I promise.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Identify yourself.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Friendly. Sarah sent me. There's a cure sample up here.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: On the shelf. I kept it cold. Take it. Don't break it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: She's infected. I'm taking it back to the lab to synthesize the antidote.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Then move. I'll hold this position. Anyone follows you, they don't get past me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah, stay with me. It's ready.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: You... actually did it. The dosage should be enough. Just... administer it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Hold still.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah, something about this outbreak doesn't add up. The strain, the timing — it feels engineered.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I've had the same thought. The pathogen's structure is too clean. Mutations don't organize themselves like this.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Engineered by who?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: There was a man — they called him the Doctor. He worked out of the outpost on the ridge before Viktor's people walled it off. If anyone designed this, it was him.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Viktor is shielding him?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Viktor needs him. The Doctor gives Viktor leverage — fear, sickness, control. Pull the Doctor out, and Viktor's grip on the island weakens.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then I need to get into that outpost. Who would know how?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Anne. Everyone passes through her store eventually. She hears things. Start there.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. I need to get inside the outpost.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Do you, now. That's a fast way to end up in a shallow grave.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I think the Doctor up there is the one who started all of this. Viktor's just the muscle around him.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: ...That tracks. People come through here whispering about lights at the outpost late at night. Crates moved up the road. Not the kind of supplies soldiers need.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then how do I get in?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: The front gate is sealed. There's a key — one of Viktor's runners dropped it in the cave by the village a week back, running from something he wouldn't talk about. Nobody's been brave enough to fetch it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll go. Anything else I should know?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Yes. Viktor patrols outpost two himself. Whatever you came for, you'll have to walk straight past him to reach the Doctor.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I'm going into the outpost. Anything I should know about the place?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Lower levels run off their own line. Independent generator. Doctor's choice, not Viktor's.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Meaning?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Meaning whoever built that setup wanted to keep working even if the village went dark. Tells you who's really in charge up there.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Good to know. Thanks, George.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Mm. Watch your back.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: You walked through my front door. That takes either nerve or stupidity. I haven't decided which.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm not here for you, Viktor. I'm here for the man behind you.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: The Doctor. Of course. Everyone eventually wants a piece of him.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You know what he did. You know he started this.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: I know what he's useful for. The village fears sickness. Fear makes people obedient. That's a fair trade.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It's not a trade. It's a leash. And he's holding the other end of yours.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: Bold words. Walk past me, then. See how far his loyalty to me runs when you're standing in his lab.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I intend to.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Ah. The variable I've been hearing about. You've made it further than I projected.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You did this. The outbreak. All of it.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: I refined a pathogen. The outbreak is simply the experiment running its course. Calling it 'mine' is sentimental.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: People are dead. Whole families.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: And yet the data is unprecedented. Legacy is rarely built on comfort.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah is close to a cure. I need whatever sample you have. The original.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: You assume I'd resist. I won't. A cure is simply the experiment's conclusion — and I am curious to see it. The sample is on the shelf. The fuse to the synthesis machine, too. Take them.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Why help me?

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Because Viktor believes he commands me. He doesn't. And because the work outlives the worker. Even yours.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It's done. I got past Viktor. The Doctor confirmed it — he made the pathogen.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: And you walked back out. That's something.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah needs the lab secured before she can finish her work. I'm going to fortify the store too — you've been a lifeline. You should still have one tomorrow.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: ...That's kind. Genuinely. Most people only think about themselves at a moment like this.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You're part of how this island survives. That matters.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Then get to it. And come back when you need information again. I'll have more by then.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. I heard Mary's boat is at the dock. Is it true?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: It's true. She came in days ago with a hole in the hull. Hasn't left since.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: If I can get her boat fixed, she'll take people off the island.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: That's the rumour. She'll need wood and a toolkit. Lucky for you, there's lumber lying around the village square — what's left of it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And Viktor?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: He's holed up at the outpost with his pet doctor. He won't like people leaving. Move quietly. And if you find Sarah at the lab — she's been close to something. Closer than anyone admits.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Thanks, Anne.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Don't thank me. Just come back through that door with a boat ride to offer.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: You bring tools or just questions?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Both. Toolkit and wood. Enough to patch her up.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Good. Hull's split on the starboard side. Two solid planks and a proper kit and she'll float.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And in return?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: I take you off this rock. You, and anyone with sense enough to come along. That's the deal.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Deal. Let's get to work.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Didn't expect company.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary's boat is seaworthy again. We're leaving the island. Come with us.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Land's been good to me. But the land's gone quiet in a bad way.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then bring what you can carry. There's room.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Alright. I'll come. Take the supplies too — no sense leaving them for the dead.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Grid's holding. Barely.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Leave it. There's a boat. Mary's. We're going.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Off the island?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Off the island. Sarah's coming. Linda's coming. Anne too if she'll move.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Then there's nothing left here worth keeping the lights on for. I'll come.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: You shouldn't be here. The doctor has eyes everywhere.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then come with us. Mary's boat is fixed. We sail tonight.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: My research —

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Bring it. Bring everything. A cure means nothing if you don't live to finish it.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: ...You're right. I'll pack what I can carry. Let's go.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: That everyone?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Everyone who wants out. Sarah, George, Linda — Anne's right behind me. Food's in the hold.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Good. Tide's turning. We don't wait.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then take us out. There's nothing left here for any of us.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Cast off. Hold on to something. The sea's the only honest thing left.
